item.title: Trump falsely alleges voting machines are "vulnerable" and "easily compromised"
item.description: President Trump alleged voting machines and ballot-counting systems are "extremely exposed to attack" — but experts say voting machines are subject to intense controls.

item.title: Fact checking Trump speech on election security
item.description: President Trump is delivering a speech on election security Thursday night at the White House.

item.title: Trump speaks on elections, alleging Chinese access to voter data
item.description: President Trump gave a primetime address on elections tonight, as he remains focused on the 2020 race.

item.title: Watch: Trump speaks on elections, alleges Chinese access to voter data | Special Report
item.description: President Trump on Thursday delivered a primetime address about election security where he railed against China. Tony Dokoupil anchored CBS News' special report.

item.title: Maps show wildfire smoke forecast, air quality alerts in swath of U.S.
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week.

item.title: U.S. concludes another night of Iran strikes amid battle over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran said it would attack "all infrastructure in the region" if President Trump follows through on his threats to attack Iranian civilian infrastructure.

item.title: Doctor on how concerning wildfire smoke is for health
item.description: Millions of Americans across the Northeast and Midwest were under air quality alerts this week as smoke from Canadian and Minnesota wildfires settled in. At the same time, some of those same areas saw severe heat. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

item.title: Sen. Mark Warner slams Trump's primetime speech: "Incredible lies"
item.description: Sen. Mark Warner joins CBS News with his reaction to President Trump's primetime address on Thursday about U.S. election security.

item.title: Trump rails against China in his speech about U.S. elections
item.description: President Trump addressed the U.S. in a primetime speech on Thursday night about election security, launching allegations against China. Tony Dokoupil anchored CBS News' special report.

item.title: Deadly helicopter crash in Hudson River likely caused by bird strike, NTSB says
item.description: A family of five from Spain, including three children, and the pilot died in the April 2025 crash.

item.title: Blanche holds meeting with Epstein accusers after Tillis' request
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche met with accusers of Jeffrey Epstein following a demand to do so by a Republican senator whose support is crucial to advancing his nomination to lead the Justice Department.

item.title: At least 2 dead in Central Texas flooding as torrential rain slams region
item.description: More than a foot of rain has fallen since Monday, triggering dangerous flash flooding​ in Central Texas.

item.title: Federal judge slams Trump, DOJ over IRS lawsuit
item.description: This week, a federal judge slammed President Trump, his lawyers, and the Justice Department over the president's lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson has more details and analysis.

item.title: Dozens of Marineland's whales coming to U.S. through rescue plan
item.description: Dozens of beluga whales are set to be relocated from the shuttered Canadian theme park to aquariums across the United States through an international emergency rescue effort, officials said.

item.title: Watch: Wildfire smoke blankets cities across U.S., Canada
item.description: Heavy smoke from wildfires in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing parts of the U.S., prompting air quality alerts in 18 states. These videos show the hazy conditions on the ground in several cities.

item.title: FDA approves new cholesterol-lowering pill, doctor explains what it does differently
item.description: The FDA has approved a new daily cholesterol pill that works differently from popular statins and may deliver better results for some people. Dr. Corey Bradley, a cardiologist at Columbia University's Irving Medical Center, joins "CBS Evening News" to discuss.

item.title: New Mexico attorney general accuses Justice Department of hindering Epstein probe
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche told senators at his confirmation hearing that the Justice Department will investigate any new leads related to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. New Mexico Attorney General Raúl Torrez accuses Blanche of stonewalling a live Epstein investigation right now, and he joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak possibly traced to lettuce from Mexico used by Taco Bell
item.description: A lettuce supplier to fast-food giant Taco Bell is being investigated as a possible source for a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak that has sickened thousands of people.

item.title: Thousands of election-focused workers cut during Trump's 2nd term
item.description: Since the beginning of his second administration, the government has cut thousands of workers who were tasked with ensuring secure elections in the U.S.

item.title: Deadly NYC helicopter crash possibly caused by bird strike, NTSB documents indicate
item.description: 15 months after a sightseeing helicopter broke up in the air and crashed in New York City's Hudson River, investigators revealed what they believe brought down the chopper, killing the pilot and a family of five. Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: Universities get funding from foreign entities on U.S. watch lists, records show
item.description: The State Department is cracking down on universities accepting funding from foreign entities on U S. government watch lists.

item.title: Teleprompter operator made over $100K betting on Trump speeches
item.description: After detecting suspicious activity on the teleprompter operator's account, Kalshi investigated and then referred the case to federal regulators.

item.title: 7/16: CBS Evening News
item.description: Air quality emergencies impact at least 21 states amid wildfires; Trump speaks on elections, alleging Chinese access to voter data.

item.title: Navy's Blue Angels won't be reprimanded after video shows low flyover
item.description: The Blue Angels said an aircraft "flew lower than standard profiles, resulting in a disturbance on the beach that affected civilian chairs and umbrellas."

item.title: 7/16: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: President Trump to give primetime speech on election security; Todd Blanche has second day of confirmation hearing for attorney general role.

item.title: Doctors question plan to screen military members for low testosterone
item.description: Doctors raised a range of concerns about a testosterone screening policy announced by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, which is likely to be costly to implement.

item.title: Airlines say permanent daylight saving time won't be simple
item.description: Adjusting to permanent daylight saving time would cause significant disruptions to schedules and operations, an airline trade group said.

item.title: Meteorite that hit home contains "alien world chemistry," experts say
item.description: When scientists examined the preserved fragments of a meteorite that crashed in 2024, they found brine-like fluids and key molecules.

item.title: The Uplift: Derby dream come true
item.description: David Begnaud meets a man who has attended the Kentucky Derby for 79 years in a row – and his dying wish to make it there one last time.

item.title: The Uplift: Desmond Bryant
item.description: David Begnaud sits down with former NFL player Desmond Bryant, who shares how he survived a dark period in his life – and came out helping others.
